SCOAS Execution Report - magento

Timestamp: 2026-01-29-11-30

Summary

2932
Rule Violations
5.27
Average Violations/Route
0
Routes Without Violations
556
Routes Analyzed
1974
Responses Analyzed
If your OpenAPI Specification file contains status code misuses, they should be fixed based on the rule descriptions that are provided. The rule sources provide references to the standards (RFCs) that were used to define the rules.

Details

  • 556 violations of the rule has-406-if-accept
  • 483 violations of the rule no-401-if-no-auth
  • 392 violations of the rule has-422-if-params
  • 333 violations of the rule has-404-if-path
  • 269 violations of the rule has-413-if-content-length
  • 269 violations of the rule has-415-if-content-type
  • 269 violations of the rule has-422-if-payload
  • 196 violations of the rule has-400-if-params
  • 134 violations of the rule has-400-if-payload
  • 21 violations of the rule has-200-or-201-or-204-if-post
  • 6 violations of the rule has-200-or-201-or-204-if-put
  • 4 violations of the rule has-200-or-204-if-delete

Violation #1

Route: DELETE /V1/addresses/{addressId}

Responses: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Parameters: addressId (path)

Rule ID: has-404-if-path

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 404 Not Found if the method contains path parameters.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.5 (404 Not Found)

Violation #2

Route: DELETE /V1/addresses/{addressId}

Responses: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Parameters: addressId (path)

Rule ID: has-406-if-accept

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 406 Not Acceptable in case the sever does not support the Accept header specified in the request. Only applies to routes that respond with content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Violation #3

Route: DELETE /V1/addresses/{addressId}

Responses: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Parameters: addressId (path)

Rule ID: has-422-if-params

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 422 Unprocessable Content if the method contains parameters (in case of invalid semantics).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)

Violation #4

Route: DELETE /V1/addresses/{addressId}

Responses: 200 OK, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Parameters: addressId (path)

Rule ID: no-401-if-no-auth

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 401 Unauthorized if the specification does not contains an authentication mechanism.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 406 Not Acceptable in case the sever does not support the Accept header specified in the request. Only applies to routes that respond with content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Route: POST /V1/sharedCatalog/{sharedCatalogId}/assignTierPrices

Responses: 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Rule ID: has-200-or-201-or-204-if-post

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 200 OK, 201 Created, or 204 No Content in a POST method.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.3.1 (200 OK) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3.2 (201 Created) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3.5 (204 No Content) | RFC 9110, Section 9.3.3 (POST)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.1 (400 Bad Request)

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 401 Unauthorized if the specification does not contains an authentication mechanism.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Violation #2913

Responses: 401 Unauthorized, 500 Internal Server Error, default (OpenAPI)

Parameters: cartId (path)

Rule ID: has-200-or-201-or-204-if-put

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 200 OK, 201 Created, or 204 No Content in a PUT method.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.3.1 (200 OK) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3.2 (201 Created) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3.5 (204 No Content) | RFC 9110, Section 9.3.4 (PUT)
